changes_summary: Addressed both significant flags. FLAG-001: Step 2 now splits the new user turn into two values — `request_user_msg` (with channel context, sent to Anthropic this turn only) and `persisted_user_msg` (clean user text, written back to history) — so stale channel context never enters the store. FLAG-002: Step 4 now enforces both a user-turn count cap AND a byte budget (`MAX_CONVERSATION_BYTES = 80_000`), evicting whole turn-groups from the front when a heavy `find_messages`/`inspect_message` payload pushes history over budget. Trimming still respects tool_use/tool_result pairing by only cutting at user-text turn boundaries. Added explicit smoke-test step to verify channel context is NOT replayed across turns. Promoted the channel-context criterion from `should` to `must` and added two new `must` criteria for the byte budget and the persist/request split.
questions: Is 80 KB the right starting byte budget for persisted history? It leaves comfortable headroom under Anthropic's per-request limit even with system prompt + tools schema, but if you'd prefer a tighter or looser cap, say so. Plan assumes 80 KB.
On exception mid-turn, the plan rolls back to pre-turn history (drops the partial turn). Confirm that's preferable to persisting whatever was committed before the failure.

success_criteria.criterion: The module-level `_conversations` store in `src/features/admin_chat/agent.py` holds native Anthropic message dicts (user text, assistant content blocks including `tool_use`, and user `tool_result` blocks), not stringified recaps.

On a follow-up turn, `messages` passed to `self.client.messages.create` is seeded from the persisted list plus the new user message — not from a `PREVIOUS CONVERSATION:` string appended to `full_message`.
Persisted user-turn entries contain only the raw `user_message` text — channel context, recent-message snapshots, and DM headers from `full_message` are NEVER written into `_conversations`.
`_trim_conversation` enforces both a user-turn count cap AND a byte-size cap (`MAX_CONVERSATION_BYTES`), evicting whole turn-groups from the front when either limit is exceeded.
Every persisted `tool_use` block has a matching `tool_result` block in the next user message — trimming never splits a pair, and exception paths never write an unpaired state.
`python -c 'from src.features.admin_chat.agent import AdminChatAgent'` succeeds with no syntax or import errors after the edit.
`clear_conversation` still fully wipes the per-user history, and `admin_chat_cog.py:401`'s call site is unchanged.
The text-only stop branch (Claude responds with text and no tool calls) appends the assistant's response to persisted history so the reply is visible on the next turn.
If a single turn's serialized size exceeds `MAX_CONVERSATION_BYTES`, the trim function preserves it rather than emptying history — sending one oversized turn is better than breaking pairing.
REPL shape check on a synthetic history with an oversized `tool_result` confirms count-trim, byte-trim, and pairing invariants all hold.
Manual live smoke test: a three-turn DM where turn 2 references turn 1's tool output and turn 3 verifies stale channel context from turn 1 is NOT replayed. No Anthropic 400.

assumptions: In-memory per-process persistence is sufficient — no disk/DB backing required.
`MAX_CONVERSATION_BYTES = 80_000` is a reasonable starting cap (~20K tokens of headroom). Tunable as a single constant if it proves too loose or too tight.
`ADMIN_MAX_CONVERSATION_LENGTH=20` / `MEMBER_MAX_CONVERSATION_LENGTH=10` now mean 'max user turns retained'. Numbers unchanged.
The abort path's existing synthetic-`tool_result`-on-skip discipline keeps `messages` API-valid; we rely on it when persisting aborted turns.
No existing tests cover `admin_chat/agent.py`, so no test updates are required.

`response.content` (Anthropic SDK content blocks) is JSON-serializable enough for `json.dumps(..., default=str)` size measurement and for direct re-submission to `messages.create` — the in-loop code at `agent.py:418` already does the latter.
Channel context in `full_message` is request-scoped only — turn N+1 will arrive with its own fresh channel context, so we never need the previous turn's context blob in history.

If a single turn alone exceeds `MAX_CONVERSATION_BYTES`, we keep it (stop trimming at `len(turn_starts) > 1`) rather than emptying history — better to send one oversized turn than to break the conversation.
